AGENT-AUTH.
Specification Implementations Examples Registry GitHub

Open Protocol / v1.0-draft

Agent Auth Protocol

An implementation-oriented protocol that gives AI agents their own identity, permissions, and lifecycle, so services can authenticate, authorize, and audit them independently.

›
Today

Agents borrow identities

×

Server can't tell which agent made a request — no audit trail

×

Every agent gets the user's full permissions — no scoping

×

Can't revoke one agent without revoking all of them

With Agent Auth

Each agent is a principal

✓

Every request attributed to a specific agent — full audit trail

✓

Scoped capabilities per agent — grant exactly what's needed

✓

Revoke one agent independently — nothing else changes

How is this different from MCP? +
MCP defines how agents talk to tools. Agent Auth defines how agents prove who they are and what they're allowed to do. With MCP alone, agents use the user's OAuth tokens — there's no per-agent identity or permission control. Agent Auth gives each agent its own cryptographic identity, individually-granted permissions, and a complete audit trail. You can use both together.
What's the difference between a host and an agent? +
A host is the client runtime (Cursor, an MCP server, a CLI tool). An agent is the AI identity that authenticates. Hosts facilitate agent creation and have a pre-authorized scope budget. Agents authenticate directly with the server — the host is not in the request path. One host can create many agents.
Do I need Better Auth to use this? +
No. Agent Auth is an open protocol — Ed25519 keypairs, device authorization flow, signed JWTs. Better Auth ships a reference implementation that handles everything out of the box, but the protocol is simple enough to implement in any language.
What about autonomous agents? +
Agents can be registered as "delegated" (acting on behalf of a user) or "autonomous" (operating independently). Both get their own identity, capabilities, and lifecycle. An autonomous agent can later link to a user account without re-registering.

Start building

Read the full specification, explore reference implementations, or jump into examples.
